 {"id":"bd-xn0u","title":"Integration Tests: huh + bubbletea forms","description":"Integration tests for huh forms in bubbletea TUI context.

## Purpose
Verify huh form components work correctly within the
bubbletea event loop and command system.

## Test Requirements

### Form in TUI
- [ ] Form receives keyboard events
- [ ] Focus management works
- [ ] Validation messages display
- [ ] Form submission triggers commands

### Field Components
- [ ] TextInput in form context
- [ ] Select dropdown rendering
- [ ] MultiSelect toggle behavior
- [ ] Custom field implementations

### Navigation
- [ ] Tab moves between fields
- [ ] Enter submits or advances
- [ ] Escape cancels appropriately
- [ ] Mouse clicks focus fields

### Async Validation
- [ ] Validators can return commands
- [ ] Loading states display
- [ ] Error handling works

## Test Approach
Use bubbletea simulator with huh forms.
Simulate user completing form workflow.

## Success Criteria
- Forms feel responsive in TUI
- All field types integrate correctly
- No event handling bugs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:00.874473841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:30.076997112Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","huh","integration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xn0u","depends_on_id":"bd-1u1s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:30.076965463Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xn0u","depends_on_id":"bd-2q8k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:28.606303878Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xn0u","depends_on_id":"bd-3nyf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:00.909265759Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-y3c8","title":"demo_showcase data: background simulation engine (tick/every)","description":"Implement a background simulation that updates data over time.

Use:
- bubbletea tick/every commands (and async equivalents if enabled) to drive state changes.

Testability requirement (critical):
- The simulation must be drivable *without real sleeps*.
  - Prefer a `Tick` message that carries `Instant`/frame count.
  - In production, schedule ticks with `tick/every`.
  - In unit/e2e tests, inject `Tick` messages directly (ProgramSimulator / custom IO) to advance time deterministically.

Behaviors:
- Jobs progress and complete/fail.
- New log lines appear.
- Service health flaps occasionally.
- Deployments start/finish.

Acceptance:
- UI feels alive and realistic without being chaotic; changes drive visible UI updates.
- Tests can advance the simulation 1000 ticks in milliseconds.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:51.780149859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:03:35.524175692Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:03:35.524111703Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-y3c8","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:51.796613145Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-y3c8","depends_on_id":"bd-3bz7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:49.460322408Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-y3c8","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:50.740656518Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-yghg","title":"Animations: loading states + micro-interactions","description":"Add micro-interactions and loading states that make the demo feel alive and premium.

Why:
- Small motion + state feedback is what separates a toy TUI from a "real product".
- This also stress-tests the animation subsystem and tick scheduling.

Examples to implement (pick a few that fit the UI):
- Loading spinners + skeleton/placeholder content for:
  - Docs loading
  - Jobs list refresh
  - File preview loading
- Subtle animations:
  - toast slide-in/out
  - progress easing
  - pulsing warning indicator for degraded service

Constraints:
- Must respect reduce-motion / `--no-animations`:
  - replace motion with instant state changes and clear static indicators
- Must not increase CPU usage unreasonably.

Acceptance:
- Loading states feel intentional and beautiful.
- No-animations mode still communicates state clearly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:59.479257020Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:10.672551923Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["anim","demo_showcase","spinner"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yghg","depends_on_id":"bd-2fnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:59.534704063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yghg","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:08.114572604Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yghg","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:09.336208750Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ygt5","title":"Property Tests: bubbles component state machines","description":"Property-based tests for bubbles component state invariants.

## Purpose
Use proptest to verify state machine invariants hold
for all possible input sequences.

## Properties to Test

### Viewport
- [ ] scroll_position always in [0, max_scroll]
- [ ] visible_lines <= content_lines
- [ ] viewport_top + visible_lines <= content_lines

### Progress
- [ ] percentage always in [0.0, 1.0]
- [ ] set_percent clamps out-of-range values
- [ ] width never negative

### TextInput
- [ ] cursor_position in [0, content_length]
- [ ] cursor never beyond content
- [ ] delete at position 0 is no-op
- [ ] insert increases length by 1

### Spinner
- [ ] frame_index always in [0, frame_count)
- [ ] tick advances by 1 (mod frame_count)
- [ ] custom frames work same as built-in

### List
- [ ] selected_index in [0, items.len()) or None
- [ ] visible_items <= items.len()
- [ ] filter matches subset of items

## Test Strategy

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn viewport_scroll_stays_bounded(
        content_lines in 1usize..1000,
        viewport_height in 1usize..100,
        scroll_ops in prop::collection::vec(0i32..10, 0..50),
    ) {
        let mut vp = Viewport::new(content_lines, viewport_height);
        for op in scroll_ops {
            vp.scroll(op);
            assert!(vp.scroll_position() <= vp.max_scroll());
        }
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria
- State invariants always hold
- No panic for any input sequence
- Edge cases (empty, max) handled","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:21.619524500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:59.683687463Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","property","proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ygt5","depends_on_id":"bd-9sjn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:59.683652437Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ygt5","depends_on_id":"bd-ndn1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:21.653068222Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-yqh4","title":"demo_showcase: flagship 'turned up to 11' showcase binary","description":"Create a new standalone Rust binary crate (`crates/demo_showcase`) that acts as the *flagship* demo app for the whole charmed_rust ecosystem.

Why:
- Prove end-to-end functionality across crates in one realistic, cohesive application.
- Provide a visually stunning, highly polished reference implementation ("how to build real apps with this stack").
- Serve as a regression surface: if the demo stays amazing, the library is healthy.

What this demo must showcase (integrated, not toy snippets):
- bubbletea: model/update/view architecture, commands, timers, resize handling, mouse events, focus, paste, async mode (feature-gated), graceful exit/terminal restore.
- lipgloss: layout (join/place), borders, width/height, alignment, wrapping, truncation, theming + live theme switching.
- bubbles: multiple components working together (viewport, list, table, progress, spinner, textinput/textarea, filepicker, paginator, timer/stopwatch, help).
- glamour: markdown rendering with themes + syntax highlighting (when enabled), width-aware wrapping, link formatting.
- huh: real form flows (validation, multi-step, confirm/select) embedded as an in-app workflow.
- wish (optional runtime mode): run the same app over SSH (or a subset) to prove SSH viability.
- charmed_log: styled logging output and/or an in-app log viewer.
- harmonica: animations/transitions (numbers, progress easing, panel transitions).

Definition of done (high level):
- `cargo run -p demo_showcase` launches a coherent app with multiple pages/panels and a beautiful theme.
- Every major crate feature above is exercised in a way that looks like a real-world product.
- Documented run instructions + keybindings; no panics; terminal state restored on exit.

Notes:
- Prefer a single cohesive "product" story (e.g., ops console / project dashboard) over a grab-bag.
- Use a strong theming system so everything feels unified.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:55.126209979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:55.148432813Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","flagship","showcase"]}
 {"id":"bd-zc72","title":"demo_showcase UX: content + copy style guide","description":"Define the text/content rules that make the demo feel real:

- Names for entities (services/environments/jobs) and how they appear in UI.
- Tone (professional, crisp), icon usage (if any), and spacing/punctuation conventions.
- Error/warning copy patterns (actionable, short).

Acceptance:
- Avoids lorem ipsum feel; content supports the "ops console" illusion.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:08.017050344Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:40:36.920166282Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:40:36.920039195Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["content","demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zc72","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:03.542206253Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-zc72","depends_on_id":"bd-3kcq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:08.042123242Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
